Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Beauveria bassiana; Escherichia coli; Arabidopsis; Tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Beauveria bassiana; Escherichia coli; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PF18631 family; cucumopine synthase C-terminal helical bundle domain; type III opine synthase; Pictet-Spenglerase
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: tetrahydro-β-carboline intermediate leading to tryptopine A and minor tryptopine B
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses L-tryptophan with methylglyoxal via a Pictet-Spengler reaction to form a tetrahydro-β-carboline intermediate that spontaneously oxidizes/aromatizes to tryptopine A and minor tryptopine B
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key biosynthetic step for tryptopine formation; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a plant growth regulator; promotes root growth at low dose; enhances plant photosynthesis and expression of photosynthesis-related genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BBA_06474 is responsible for tryptopine synthesis; its product tryptopine A promotes Arabidopsis root growth and elevates net photosynthesis rate in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts induced by plants; silenced under laboratory cultivation conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plant-fungal interaction/plant induction; laboratory culture conditions suppress native production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Arabidopsis seedlings treated with tryptopine A; data available in Supplementary Table S6
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with purified His6-tagged protein; LC-MS/HPLC; product isolation and NMR; gene knockout and overexpression in Beauveria bassiana; plant metabolite bioassays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzymatic product is a tetrahydro-β-carboline that spontaneously oxidizes and decarboxylates to tryptopine A/B without cofactor; overexpression but not knockout showed tryptopine production in lab culture because native gene is plant-induced
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High tryptopine A dose (50 μM) inhibited Arabidopsis seedling growth by about 35%
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering fungal or heterologous production of tryptopine A and for agricultural deployment of the metabolite to enhance photosynthesis/productivity, but dosage control and biosafety evaluation are needed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BBA_06474 from Beauveria bassiana encodes a fungal PF18631/type III opine synthase. 2. It catalyzes condensation of L-tryptophan and methylglyoxal to generate tryptopine A as the major product and tryptopine B as a minor product. 3. Plant-induced expression and overexpression analyses support its in vivo role in tryptopine biosynthesis. 4. The resulting metabolite tryptopine A promotes plant growth and enhances photosynthesis-related gene expression and net photosynthetic rate.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PF18631 family; cucumopine synthase C-terminal helical bundle domain; type III opine synthase; Pictet-Spenglerase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of tryptopine A and tryptopine B from L-tryptophan and methylglyoxal following heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tryptopine A acts as a plant growth regulator and photosynthesis enhancer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Rhiz-OSase confers tryptopine A/B production in E. coli, showing homologous fungal enzymes can synthesize the bioactive metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis; heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC analysis of products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a BBA_06474 homolog with >60% amino acid identity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for heterologous biosynthesis of tryptopine A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rhiz-OSase is a fungal BBA_06474 homolog from Rhizodiscina lignyota. 2. Heterologous expression in E. coli produced tryptopine A and B. 3. This supports conservation of tryptopine synthase activity among fungal PF18631 proteins.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PF18631 family; cucumopine synthase C-terminal helical bundle domain; type III opine synthase; Pictet-Spenglerase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of tryptopine A and tryptopine B from L-tryptophan and methylglyoxal following heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tryptopine A acts as a plant growth regulator and photosynthesis enhancer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Purp-OSase confers tryptopine A/B production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis; heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC analysis of products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a BBA_06474 homolog with >60% amino acid identity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for heterologous biosynthesis of tryptopine A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purp-OSase is a fungal homolog of BBA_06474 from Purpureocillium takamizusanense. 2. Heterologous expression in E. coli produced tryptopine A and B. 3. This supports that fungal PF18631 homologs broadly encode tryptopine-forming opine synthases.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PF18631 family; cucumopine synthase C-terminal helical bundle domain; type III opine synthase; Pictet-Spenglerase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of tryptopine A and tryptopine B from L-tryptophan and methylglyoxal; also validated by in vitro assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tryptopine A acts as a plant growth regulator and photosynthesis enhancer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Asper-OSase confers tryptopine A/B production, validating conserved catalytic activity among fungal homologs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis; heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC analysis; in vitro biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly included in in vitro assay section together with BBA_06474
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for microbial production of tryptopine A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Asper-OSase from Aspergillus heteromorphus is a fungal PF18631 homolog. 2. Heterologous expression and biochemical assays showed synthesis of tryptopine A and B from L-tryptophan and methylglyoxal. 3. The study demonstrates conserved tryptopine synthase activity in diverse fungal opine synthases.
